A monumental, incredibly historic, and deeply anxious geopolitical transition occurred in East Asia. The massive escalation drew fierce, heavy criticism from international human rights organizations regarding the militarization of the war on drugs.

## SECTION 8 — This Day in History (World)

On May 20, 1927, Charles Lindbergh, a massive, highly daring American aviator, aggressively took off from Roosevelt Field in New York aboard his highly modified monoplane, the *Spirit of St. Louis*. Thirty-three and a half incredibly exhausting hours later, he successfully and aggressively landed in Paris, France, becoming the absolute very first person in human history to complete a massive, solo, nonstop transatlantic flight. His massive triumph absolutely electrified the globe and fundamentally launched the massive, modern era of commercial aviation.

On May 20, 1498, Vasco da Gama, the incredibly massive, highly ambitious Portuguese explorer, achieved an absolute, monumental milestone in global maritime history. Following a massive, perilous journey around the southern tip of Africa, his fleet officially and aggressively arrived at Calicut (Kozhikode) on the Malabar Coast of India. This incredibly massive naval achievement established the absolute, very first direct sea route from Europe to Asia, fundamentally destroying the massive Arab monopoly on the highly lucrative spice trade and aggressively launching the era of European global imperialism.

## SECTION 9 — This Day in History (India)

On May 20, 1900, Sumitranandan Pant, one of the most massive, highly revered, and deeply profound Hindi poets of the 20th century, was born in Kausani. He was an absolute, foundational pillar of the *Chhayavaad* (neo-romantic) movement in Hindi literature. His incredibly massive, brilliant body of work, heavily inspired by the breathtaking natural beauty of the Himalayas and later influenced by massive Marxist and Aurobindonian philosophy, heavily secured his absolute legacy as a towering literary giant of modern India.

On May 20, 1932, Bipin Chandra Pal, the massive, incredibly militant, and fiercely uncompromising Indian nationalist, passed away. As one-third of the absolute, legendary "Lal Bal Pal" triumvirate, he aggressively championed the massive *Swadeshi* movement and fiercely advocated for the absolute boycott of British goods. His incredibly massive, aggressive oratory and uncompromising demand for absolute independence (*Purna Swaraj*) heavily radicalized the massive Indian independence struggle, deeply shifting the movement away from moderate constitutional agitation.
